title_en: Importance for Students and Available Editions
title_id: Pentingnya bagi Para Pelajar dan Edisi-Edisi yang Tersedia
content_en:
The Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra is essential reading for students of Islamic law, particularly those studying within the Shafi'i tradition or conducting research on it. For Shafi'i fiqh students, the work contextualizes the major works they study by showing who wrote them, what intellectual debates they participated in, and how they relate to the broader trajectory of the school's development. Understanding Al-Nawawi better, for instance, requires knowing who his teachers were, what intellectual environment shaped him, and how his work was received — all questions the Tabaqat al-Kubra addresses.

For students of hadith sciences, the work is valuable because it documents the relationship between hadith scholarship and jurisprudence within the Shafi'i tradition. Many major Shafi'i scholars were simultaneously important hadith transmitters, and the Tabaqat al-Kubra records their isnad chains and scholarly assessments of their reliability, providing information that supplements the specialized rijal literature.

Students of Islamic theology benefit from the work's documentation of how Ash'ari kalam was taught and developed within a major legal school. The debates over divine attributes, human free will, and the nature of prophetic authority that run through the theological tradition can be traced in concrete biographical form through the Tabaqat al-Kubra.

For researchers in Islamic intellectual history, the work is a primary source of first importance. Its scale and comprehensiveness make it one of the most complete records of any major Islamic legal school's development that survives from the medieval period. Researchers regularly cite it as a source for information about specific scholars, their works, and their dates.

The standard modern edition is the ten-volume set edited by Mahmud Muhammad at-Tanahi and Abd al-Fattah Muhammad al-Hilw, published by Hajr in Cairo (first edition 1992–1994; reprinted multiple times). This edition is based on careful manuscript consultation and includes helpful indexes. Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyyah in Beirut has published a more accessible but less critical edition. The work has not been fully translated into any European language, though scholarly articles and monographs on specific sections have appeared in French and English. Students working without Arabic typically rely on secondary sources that synthesize material from the Tabaqat al-Kubra, but the work repays direct engagement for those with the necessary linguistic preparation.
content_id:
Thabaqat asy-Syafi'iyyah al-Kubra adalah bacaan wajib bagi para pelajar hukum Islam, terutama mereka yang belajar dalam tradisi Syafi'i atau melakukan penelitian tentangnya. Bagi para pelajar fikih Syafi'i, karya ini mengontekstualisasikan karya-karya utama yang mereka pelajari dengan menunjukkan siapa yang menulisnya, debat-debat intelektual apa yang mereka ikuti, dan bagaimana hubungan mereka dengan trajektori yang lebih luas dari perkembangan mazhab. Memahami an-Nawawi dengan lebih baik, misalnya, memerlukan pengetahuan tentang siapa guru-gurunya, lingkungan intelektual apa yang membentuknya, dan bagaimana karyanya diterima, semua pertanyaan yang dijawab Thabaqat al-Kubra.

Bagi para pelajar ilmu-ilmu hadis, karya ini berharga karena mendokumentasikan hubungan antara keilmuan hadis dan fikih dalam tradisi Syafi'i. Banyak ulama Syafi'i utama sekaligus merupakan perawi-perawi hadis yang penting, dan Thabaqat al-Kubra mencatat rantai-rantai sanad dan penilaian-penilaian keilmuan tentang keandalan mereka, menyediakan informasi yang melengkapi literatur rijal yang terspesialisasi.

Para pelajar aqidah Islam mendapat manfaat dari dokumentasi karya ini tentang bagaimana ilmu kalam Asy'ariyah diajarkan dan dikembangkan dalam sebuah mazhab hukum utama. Perdebatan-perdebatan tentang sifat-sifat ilahi, kehendak bebas manusia, dan hakikat otoritas kenabian yang mengalir melalui tradisi aqidah dapat ditelusuri dalam bentuk biografi yang konkret melalui Thabaqat al-Kubra.

Bagi para peneliti sejarah intelektual Islam, karya ini adalah sumber primer yang sangat penting. Skala dan kelengkapannya menjadikannya salah satu rekam jejak paling lengkap dari perkembangan mazhab hukum Islam mana pun yang bertahan dari masa abad pertengahan. Para peneliti secara rutin mengutipnya sebagai sumber untuk informasi tentang ulama-ulama spesifik, karya-karya mereka, dan tanggal-tanggal mereka.

Edisi modern standar adalah set sepuluh jilid yang diedit oleh Mahmud Muhammad at-Thanahi dan Abd al-Fattah Muhammad al-Hulw, diterbitkan oleh Hajr di Kairo (edisi pertama 1992-1994; dicetak ulang berkali-kali). Edisi ini berdasarkan konsultasi manuskrip yang cermat dan mencakup indeks-indeks yang berguna. Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyyah di Beirut menerbitkan edisi yang lebih mudah diakses namun kurang kritis. Karya ini belum sepenuhnya diterjemahkan ke dalam bahasa Eropa mana pun, meskipun artikel-artikel ilmiah dan monograf-monograf tentang bagian-bagian spesifik telah muncul dalam bahasa Prancis dan Inggris. Para pelajar yang bekerja tanpa bahasa Arab biasanya mengandalkan sumber-sumber sekunder yang mensintesis materi dari Thabaqat al-Kubra, namun karya ini membalas keterlibatan langsung bagi mereka yang memiliki persiapan linguistik yang diperlukan.
content_ar: The Tabaqat ash-Shafi'iyyah al-Kubra is الجوهري reading for students of Islamic law, particularly those studying within the الشافعي التراث or conducting research on it. For الشافعي الفقه students, the work contextualizes the major works they study by showing who wrote them, what intellectual debates they participated in, and how they relate to the broader trajectory of the school's development. الفهم النووي better, على سبيل المثال, requires knowing who his teachers were, what intellectual environment shaped him, and how his work was received — all questions the Tabaqat al-Kubra addresses. For students of الحديث sciences, the work is valuable لأن it documents the relationship between الحديث scholarship and jurisprudence within the الشافعي التراث. Many major الشافعي العلماء were simultaneously المهم الحديث transmitters, and the Tabaqat al-Kubra records their الإسناد chains and العلمي assessments of their reliability, providing information that supplements the specialized rijal literature. Students of Islamic theology benefit from the work's documentation of how الأشعري علم الكلام was taught and developed within a major legal school. The debates over divine attributes, human free will, and the nature of prophetic authority that run through the theological التراث can be traced in concrete biographical form through the Tabaqat al-Kubra. For researchers in Islamic intellectual history, the work is a الأساسي source of أولاً importance. Its scale and comprehensiveness make it one of the most complete records of any major Islamic legal school's development that survives from the medieval period. Researchers regularly cite it as a source for information about specific العلماء, their works, and their dates. The standard modern edition is the ten-volume set edited by Mahmud محمد ﷺ at-Tanahi and Abd al-Fattah محمد ﷺ al-Hilw, published by Hajr in Cairo (أولاً edition 1992–1994; reprinted multiple times). This edition is based on careful manuscript consultation and includes helpful indexes. Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyyah in Beirut has published a more accessible but less البالغ الأهمية edition. The work has not been fully translated into any European language, though العلمي articles and monographs on specific sections have appeared in French and English. Students working without Arabic typically rely on secondary sources that synthesize material from the Tabaqat al-Kubra, but the work repays direct engagement for those with the necessary linguistic preparation.
